title: festuca asperifolia
intro: Festuca asperifolia, also known as rough fescue, is a perennial grass native to North America, characterized by its tufted growth habit and rough, hairy leaves. Traditionally, Festuca asperifolia has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is believed to have several health benefits, including soothing digestive issues, reducing fever, and supporting respiratory health. Festuca asperifolia can be prepared into various herbal products, such as teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to harness its medicinal properties.
